revision.text:
'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ・まくたのくにのみやつこ・うまくたこくぞう・まくたこくぞう)は[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[天津彦根|天津彦根命]]。[[成務天皇|成務]]朝に[[茨城国造]][[建許呂命]]の子の[[深河意弥命]]が馬来田国造になったという。
=== 氏族 === 不明。
=== 本拠 === 上総国[[望陀郡]]。
=== 氏神 === 不明。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|うまくたのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:上総国|うまくたのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:千葉県の歴史|うまくたのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:古墳時代|うまくたのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代|うまくたのくにのみやつこ]]

=== 本拠 === 上総国[[望陀郡]]。

'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ、まくたのくにのみやつこ、うまくたこくぞう、まくたこくぞう）は、[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[成務天皇]]朝に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖とされる[[建許呂命]]の子の[[深河意弥命]]が馬来田国造に定められたのに始まるという。
=== 氏族 === 不明。

=== 氏神 === 不明。

'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ、まくたのくにのみやつこ、うまくたこくぞう、まくたこくぞう）は、[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[成務天皇]]朝に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖とされる[[建許呂命]]の子の[[深河意弥命]]が馬来田国造に定められたのに始まるという。
=== 氏族 === 不明。
=== 本拠 === 上総国[[望陀郡]]。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の[[木更津市]]内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。
=== 氏神 === 不明。

== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[成務天皇]]朝に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖とされる[[建許呂命]]の子の深河意弥命が馬来田国造に定められたのに始まるという。

=== 本拠 === 上総国[[望陀郡]]。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の[[木更津市]]内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。

'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ、まくたのくにのみやつこ、うまくたこくぞう、まくたこくぞう）は、[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[成務天皇]]朝に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖とされる[[建許呂命]]の子の深河意弥命が馬来田国造に定められたのに始まるという。
=== 氏族 === 不明。
=== 本拠 === 上総国[[望陀郡]]。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の[[木更津市]]内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。
== 氏神 == 不明。
=== 墓 === *'''[[飯籠塚古墳]]'''（いいごづかこふん） *:[[千葉県]][[君津市]]にある全長102メートルの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]の築造と見られる。 *'''[[箕輪浅間神社古墳|浅間神社古墳]]'''（せんげんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長105メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[白山神社古墳 (君津市)|白山神社古墳]]'''（はくさんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長89メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]'''（ぎおんながすがこふんぐん） *:千葉県[[木更津市]]にある古墳群で、[[5世紀]]半ばから[[7世紀]]にかけて築造された。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html 房総の古墳を歩く 馬来田国の古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:上総国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:千葉県の歴史]]

'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ、まくたのくにのみやつこ、うまくたこくぞう、まくたこくぞう）は、[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」には、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]の子・'''深河意弥命'''が国造に定められたのに始まるという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[膳大伴部氏]]'''（かしわでのおおともべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）。[[三上氏]]族の一派。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''馬来田国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[上総国]]'''[[望陀郡]]'''、現在の[[袖ヶ浦市]]全域、[[木更津市]]の大部分、[[君津市]]と[[鴨川市]]の一部に相当する。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の[[木更津市]]内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。
== 氏神 == 不明。
=== 墓 === *'''[[飯籠塚古墳]]'''（いいごづかこふん） *:[[千葉県]][[君津市]]にある全長102メートルの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]の築造と見られる。 *'''[[箕輪浅間神社古墳|浅間神社古墳]]'''（せんげんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長105メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[白山神社古墳 (君津市)|白山神社古墳]]'''（はくさんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長89メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]'''（ぎおんながすがこふんぐん） *:千葉県[[木更津市]]にある古墳群で、[[5世紀]]半ばから[[7世紀]]にかけて築造された。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html 房総の古墳を歩く 馬来田国の古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:上総国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:千葉県の歴史]]

=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''馬来田国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[上総国]]'''[[望陀郡]]'''、現在の[[袖ケ浦市]]全域、[[木更津市]]の大部分、[[君津市]]と[[鴨川市]]の一部に相当する。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の木更津市内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。
== 氏神 == 不明。
=== 墓 === *'''[[飯籠塚古墳]]'''（いいごづかこふん） *:[[千葉県]][[君津市]]にある全長102メートルの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]の築造と見られる。 *'''[[箕輪浅間神社古墳|浅間神社古墳]]'''（せんげんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長105メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[白山神社古墳 (君津市)|白山神社古墳]]'''（はくさんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長89メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]'''（ぎおんながすがこふんぐん） *:千葉県[[木更津市]]にある古墳群で、[[5世紀]]半ばから[[7世紀]]にかけて築造された。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html 房総の古墳を歩く 馬来田国の古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:上総国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:千葉県の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]
[[Category:History-related stubs in Japan]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Chiba Prefecture]] [[ja:馬来田国造]]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of burial mounds in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=draft}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=draft}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=draft}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=draft}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=draft}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市|12=draft}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市|12=draft}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=draft}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=draft}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:馬来田国造 up to rev 89722609
revision.text:
'''馬来田国造'''（うまくたのくにのみやつこ、まくたのくにのみやつこ、うまくたこくぞう、まくたこくぞう）は、[[上総国]]中西部を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」には、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[天津彦根命]]の子孫で[[茨城国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]の子・'''深河意弥命'''が国造に定められたのに始まるという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[膳大伴部氏]]'''（かしわでのおおともべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''馬来田国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[上総国]]'''[[望陀郡]]'''、現在の[[袖ケ浦市]]全域、[[木更津市]]の大部分、[[君津市]]と[[鴨川市]]の一部に相当する。[[金鈴塚古墳]]など[[小櫃川]]下流域の木更津市内にある[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]は、馬来田国造が造営したと考えられている。
== 氏神 == 不明。
=== 墓 === *'''[[飯籠塚古墳]]'''（いいごづかこふん） *:[[千葉県]][[君津市]]にある全長102メートルの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]の築造と見られる。 *'''[[箕輪浅間神社古墳|浅間神社古墳]]'''（せんげんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長105メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[白山神社古墳 (君津市)|白山神社古墳]]'''（はくさんじんじゃこふん） *:千葉県君津市にある全長89メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造と見られる。 *'''[[祇園・長須賀古墳群]]'''（ぎおんながすがこふんぐん） *:千葉県[[木更津市]]にある古墳群で、[[5世紀]]半ばから[[7世紀]]にかけて築造された。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html 房総の古墳を歩く 馬来田国の古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うまくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:上総国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:千葉県の歴史]]

== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=draft|12=draft}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市|12=draft|12=draft}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=draft|12=draft}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa Province|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|ja|袖ケ浦市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodegaura|qq=}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisarazu|qq=}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|ja|君津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimitsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Kamogawa|ja|鴨川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamogawa|qq=}}. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.text:
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
