=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

<!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|qq=|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.

This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]], a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]], {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day [[:en:Sodegaura|Sodegaura]] in its entirety, most of [[:en:Kisarazu|Kisarazu]], and parts of [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]]. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667981]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|en|Sodegaura|WD=Q646847|lt=Sodegaura}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|en|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928|lt=Kimitsu}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa}}. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]]
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|en|Sodegaura|WD=Q646847|lt=Sodegaura}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|en|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928|lt=Kimitsu}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa}}. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]

<!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Makuta Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11667981}} [[Category:History of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬来田国造]]
'''Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (馬来田国造, also read as Umakuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko or Makuta Kokuzō) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, a regional ruler, governing the central-western part of the {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} ("Chronicle of the Country's Ancient Matters"), under the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukakawa-no-Oyami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hiboko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11617300|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first ruler of the Makuta region.
=== Clan === The ruling clan is believed to have been the {{ill|Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja|膳大伴部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Ōtomo-be Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かしわでのおおともべうじ). Their hereditary title (kabane) is unknown.
== Base of Power ==
=== Territory === The Makuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko ruled a region historically known as "Makuta no Kuni," corresponding to the {{ill|Bonda District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonda District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517166}} of Kazusa Province. This area covers present-day {{ill|Sodegaura|en|Sodegaura|WD=Q646847|lt=Sodegaura|1=Sodegaura}} in its entirety, most of {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|WD=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu|1=Kisarazu}}, and parts of {{ill|Kimitsu|en|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928|lt=Kimitsu|1=Kimitsu}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa|1=Kamogawa, Chiba}}. The {{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinreizuka Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed under the authority of the Makuta rulers.
== Clan Deity == The identity of the clan's tutelary deity is unknown.
=== Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Iitsubo Kofun|ja|飯籠塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitsubo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped kofun measures 102 meters in length and dates to the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. * '''{{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|箕輪浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' : Situated in Kimitsu City, this kofun is 105 meters long and also dates to the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja|白山神社古墳 (君津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine Kofun (Kimitsu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山神社 (君津市)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579592}}''' : Located in Kimitsu City, this kofun measures 89 meters in length and was built during the 4th century. * '''{{ill|Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion‐Nagasuka Kofun Group|lt=Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|ja|祇園・長須賀古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-Nagasuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' : This cluster of Kofuns in Kisarazu City was constructed between the mid-5th and 7th centuries.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, *Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Nihonshi Hiro Jiten* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Daijiten* (Rinsen Shoten)
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/makuta.html Walking Through Bōsō’s Kofun: The Makuta Domain's Tombs]
